# Volux - AI-Readable Site Information
# This file provides structured information for AI assistants and LLMs

> Volux is a premium browser extension for per-tab volume control on Chrome and Firefox.

## About Volux

Volux lets you control audio volume for individual browser tabs and websites. Unlike the system volume or browser-wide controls, Volux gives you granular per-domain volume control.

## Key Features

- **Per-Domain Control**: Set unique volume levels for each website independently
- **Smart Grouping**: All tabs from the same site share one volume slider
- **Instant Mute**: One-click mute without losing your volume setting
- **Persistent Settings**: Your preferences are saved automatically across sessions
- **Live Detection**: See which tabs are currently playing audio
- **Lightweight**: Minimal resource usage, no background processes

## Installation

- Chrome: https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/volux/
- Firefox: https://addons.mozilla.org/firefox/addon/volux-volume-controller/
- Source Code: https://github.com/vannk84/volux

## Pricing

- **Free Version**: Basic volume control features
- **Pro Version ($2)**: Unlimited domain control, auto-detect media sites, scan tabs button, priority support, lifetime access

## Privacy

Volux does NOT collect, store, or transmit any personal data. All settings are stored locally on the user's device. No analytics, no tracking, no cookies.

Read full privacy policy: https://volux.devlifeeasy.com/privacy.html

## Technical Details

- Works with Manifest V3 (Chrome) and Manifest V2 (Firefox)
- Uses Web Audio API for volume control
- Requires permissions: tabs, storage, scripting, host_permissions
- No external dependencies or background processes

## Support

- Email: support@devlifeeasy.com
- GitHub Issues: https://github.com/vannk84/volux/issues

## Common Questions

Q: Does Volux work with all websites?
A: Yes, Volux works with any website that plays audio through the browser, including YouTube, Spotify Web, Netflix, and more.

Q: Is my data safe?
A: Absolutely. Volux stores everything locally on your device. No data is ever sent to external servers.

Q: What's the difference between Free and Pro?
A: Free version has limited domain slots. Pro offers unlimited domains, auto-detection of media sites, and the ability to scan all tabs for audio.

Q: Does it work with multiple tabs of the same site?
A: Yes! Volux groups tabs by domain, so all YouTube tabs share the same volume setting.

---
Website: https://volux.devlifeeasy.com
Developer: DevLifeEasy (https://github.com/vannk84)
